title: Intermediate variables for function calls + expression coverage
body:
Follow up to #6135

I was able to track down my `V3Broken` problem to the fact that class `random()` calls don't know they are impure until after `V3Random`.  I'm currently doing `clearCacheTree()` but perhaps I should be more surgical.

After this `t_cover_expr` is working but `t_cover_line_expr_cc` (which uses the same Verilog) is running into a similar `V3Broken` problem.  This time it's on the `NodeFTask` instead of the `NodeFTaskRef`.  And `getPurityRecurse()` is returning false because `AstCoverInc` is not pure.  This confuses me somewhat.  I get that `AstCoverInc` is perturbing the coverage state, so yeah, a function that has one of them is not pure as far as the generated C++ goes.  But coverage isn't a thing described by the Verilog.  So from an RTL perspective the function in question is pure:
```
    function automatic bit invert(bit x);
        return ~x;
    endfunction
```
So what should `AstNode::isPure()` mean?  Does it mean that the C++ function is pure or that the Verilog function is pure?

Clearing again in `V3Coverage` "fixes" the `V3Broken` problem but then `invert()` is marked as impure and ineligible for expression coverage which is not true.
